name: Cost-Optimized AI Gateway
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure API Management
services.purpose: Central AI gateway — rate limiting, token budgets, model routing, analytics

services.name: Azure OpenAI (Primary)
services.purpose: GPT-4o primary endpoint for complex reasoning and high-quality responses

services.name: Azure OpenAI (Secondary)
services.purpose: GPT-4o-mini secondary endpoint for simple queries and cost optimization

prod.note: 60% of traffic routed here for simple tasks

services.name: Azure Functions
services.purpose: Request classifier — routes queries to optimal model based on complexity

services.name: Azure Cache for Redis
services.purpose: Semantic response caching — dedup repeated queries, reduce AI API calls

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Usage tracking — per-tenant token consumption, billing, quota enforcement

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: API keys for multiple OpenAI endpoints, tenant secrets, certificates

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Cost analytics — per-tenant spend, model routing effectiveness, cache hit rate

optimization_tips: Route 60-70% of simple queries to GPT-4o-mini — saves 90% per token vs GPT-4o
Enable semantic caching in Redis — 30-50% cache hit rate reduces AI costs significantly
Set per-tenant token quotas in Cosmos DB — prevents runaway costs from heavy users
Use APIM consumption tier for dev — pays only per API call, no base cost
Implement prompt compression to reduce input tokens by 30-50% before routing to AI
Monitor cache hit rates and adjust TTL — stale cache wastes storage, expired cache wastes AI calls
